characters.name: Shàun Pàlajé
characters.tagline: the Child of Destiny who founded the Instructional Nation
characters.quote: I will not waver again. I choose a strong Kàlavé. Here and now, I declare the dissolution of the Kàlavatan Republic and the founding of the Kàlavé Instructional Nation. 'Ï'E'A Géjéné Kuējebeyï.
characters.title: First First Instructor of the Kàlavé Instructional Nation
characters.body: Born in 1899. Born as the “Child of Destiny” fated to found an Instructional Nation for the first time since the eighteenth century, he broke free from the Republic’s bondage and turned to Instructionalism. He possesses both immense strength and consummate sword skill.
characters.combat_style: Stance of Forthrightness / Duïlé sword · fox-colored knife
characters.comment: Koto: “The great first Highness! He is also my lord’s root and origin!”

characters.name: Káin Kàna
characters.tagline: the unconventional foster father
characters.quote: Let’s call it ‘results first.’ Do as much as you can.
characters.title: Third President of the Kàlavatan Republic; master of Kïnàiny
characters.body: A master of Duïlé who took on the role of father to Pàlajé. He later became the third president, but was assassinated by Nine Earths. He taught Pàlajé kindness and freedom.
characters.comment: Arsha: “He was quite the unconventional president. He was also the first owner of the pure-white knife.”

characters.name: Ládén Kïnàbé
characters.tagline: Pàlajé’s foster brother and best friend
characters.quote: Look to the future, Pàlajé!
characters.title: Deputy master of Kïnàiny; Council member
characters.body: Kàna’s nephew and Pàlajé’s foster brother. To prevent the birth of the Instructional Nation, he clashed with Pàlajé at the Bridge of Conversion and lost his life. He was Lai’s master.
characters.combat_style: Stance of Gentleness, Stance of No-Mind / sword · Pure-White Knife
characters.comment: Koto: “Is he one of my lord’s ancestors? To stand against His Highness Pàlajé is no small feat.”

characters.name: Èshe
characters.tagline: the village girl who was Pàlajé’s first love
characters.quote: You are the Child of Destiny. I’m only a poor village girl. And I want to repay the relatives and the village that raised me.
characters.title: Village girl
characters.body: A woman Pàlajé and Kïnàbé met on the road to Xāváléqï. Bound by the customs of Làitéqï Village, she was to marry a landlord and turned down Pàlajé’s invitation.
characters.comment: Arsha: “It is no exaggeration to say that her choice moved history.”

characters.name: Ahhárén Xàsané
characters.tagline: the Shadow Owl who rose from poverty
characters.quote: What Kàlavé needs, is a ruler with a heart.
characters.title: “Shadow Owl” of Nine Earths; former Kïnàiny master
characters.body: As the Shadow Owl of Nine Earths, he abducted Pàlajé and demanded the founding of an Instructional Nation. Once a member of Kïnàiny, he rebelled after the truth behind Kàna’s assassination was covered up.
characters.comment: Koto: “He is a user of the Stance of Savagery. I feel a bit of kinship with him myself.”

characters.name: Shàun Pàlé
characters.tagline: the White Owl who paved the road to the Instructional Nation
characters.quote: If you want to kill me, then do it. May fortune bless your choice.
characters.title: Leader of the Ismizlark Alliance; President of the Kàlavatan Republic; “White Owl”
characters.body: He tempted Pàlajé onward, secured emergency powers, and laid the foundation of the Instructional Nation. He then took his own life and framed the Council for the president’s assassination.
characters.comment: Arsha: “A strategist to a frightening degree. To have even mastered the True Stance of Balance...”

characters.name: Shàun Lèi
characters.tagline: the woman who loved Pàlajé and gave him everything
characters.quote: I shall love you forever, Child of Destiny.
characters.title: Pàlajé’s secretary; intelligence operative of Nine Earths
characters.body: Pàlajé’s secretary and, at the same time, Pàlé’s spy. She schemed from the shadows to fulfill Pàlajé’s “destiny,” and later used their twin sons to launch a coup.
characters.comment: Koto: “That readiness to stop at nothing for the one she loves—I find it deeply relatable!”

characters.name: Lakán Lài
characters.tagline: the moderate swordsman who values dialogue
characters.quote: It is. The future is waiting.
characters.title: Speaker of Parliament; commander of the Confederated States Army
characters.body: A man trained by Kïnàbé who inherited the Pure-White blade. Together with Àkané, he slew Àya, and later guided the country as Speaker of Parliament.
characters.combat_style: Stance of No-Mind, Stance of Moderation: Prelude / Pure-White Knife
characters.comment: Arsha: “That strength not to give up on discussion is something I would like to learn from as a merchant too.”

characters.name: Bïláin Àkané
characters.tagline: the sacrificed half of the twins
characters.quote: You’re the one who took Nèi, Mother, and Father from me!
characters.title: Second First Instructor; direct bishop of Seran
characters.body: Born as the son of Pàlajé and Lei, but given to the Bïláin house as an adopted child because one of a pair of twins was deemed accursed. He lost his family in Àya’s coup and struck back alongside Lai.
characters.comment: Koto: “Having the same face as the fake Àkané makes things rather confusing.”

characters.name: Shàun Àya
characters.tagline: the twin who killed his father and stole the throne
characters.quote: Here dies His Highness the First Instructor who knew no hesitation. From this day onward, I will choose everything.
characters.title: False Bïláin Àkané; “Shadow Owl” of Nine Earths
characters.body: Àkané’s twin brother. Reared under Lei’s education and drug regimen, he assassinated his father Pàlajé and usurped the office of First Instructor.
characters.comment: Arsha: “A dictator who tried to choose everything with his own hands. It seems the price of the medicine was great indeed.”

characters.name: Yïmádén Rià
characters.tagline: a legendary swordmaster and fine teacher
characters.quote: I’ll give you a passing mark. Leave the rear guard to me.
characters.title: Leader of the Xàsané faction of Nine Earths; swordmaster
characters.body: The master who led the Xàsané faction of Nine Earths and taught the sword to Lai and Àkané. In later ages as well, his name was famed as that of a legendary swordsman.
characters.combat_style: Stance of Moderation (applied form) / bamboo sword · sword
characters.comment: Koto: “So he is a master swordsman. I wonder which is superior, him or my automatic counterattack?”

characters.name: Sāna
characters.tagline: the liberal who supports the rebel alliance
characters.quote: To prepare for Àya’s “next move” when it comes, now is the time for the bishops to deliberate in Seran.
characters.title: Direct bishop of Seran
characters.body: A liberal of Seran. She supported Lai and Àkané as a trusted ally.
characters.comment: Arsha: “A capable person who lays the groundwork properly behind the scenes of politics.”

characters.name: Yanan Kau (Asásén Pàlau)
characters.tagline: the terrorist who serves as Àya’s puppet
characters.quote: My name is Yanan Kau. In the name of the laws of Ismizlark, Dírál branch, I will bring down the hammer of justice upon you.
characters.title: Leader of the Kau faction of Nine Earths
characters.body: As leader of the Kau faction, he carried out numerous terrorist attacks. He enhanced his body with drugs and fought Nekinei, Lai, and the others.
characters.comment: Koto: “Relying on medicine is not beautiful.”

characters.name: Èn Nèkïné
characters.tagline: the police officer with genius swordsmanship
characters.quote: It will. The future is waiting.
characters.title: Chief of First Investigations, National Police Agency; commander of the anti-terror unit
characters.body: A genius swordswoman serving in the police. She developed the “Stance of Moderation: Kai,” combining Duïlé with riot-control techniques, and fought beside Lai and Àkané.
characters.combat_style: Stance of Moderation: Kai / riot-control baton arts · Duïlé knife
characters.comment: Arsha: “That police young lady is quite capable. The Revised Stance of Balance is practical indeed.”

characters.name: Tïràun Paté
characters.tagline: the incompetent sovereign manipulated by foreign powers
characters.quote: Rest easy, Rià. In the name of the Tïràun house, your deeds shall be exempt from punishment.
characters.title: Third First Instructor
characters.body: A delinquent youth of the Nine Clans. Set up as First Instructor as Worl’s puppet, he tried to flee into exile after demands that he resign the office, and died in what was called an accident—an assassination in all but name.
characters.comment: Koto: “That thing being the First Instructor is an insult to my lord!”

characters.name: Hánén Kïlàné
characters.tagline: the compromise-minded pacifist
characters.quote: It is decided. Cool your head a little.
characters.title: Fourth First Instructor
characters.body: A conciliatory leader who took office after Patei. Tané’s father-in-law. He loved his daughter Mono, but was assassinated by the schemes of Ètïré.
characters.comment: Arsha: “He was a steady politician, but it seems the radicals found him lacking.”

characters.name: Shàun Akau
characters.tagline: the White Owl who loved Tané and took darkness upon herself
characters.quote: It is my mission to stake my life on protecting His Holiness the First Instructor!
characters.title: Administrative officer; press officer; “White Owl”
characters.body: Àya’s daughter, though she was raised as a boy. She fell deeply in love with Tané, stained her own hands so he might become First Instructor, and became the White Owl. Later she served as an administrative officer.
characters.comment: Koto: “That woman! To beguile my lord—I will never forgive her!”

characters.name: Apàla
characters.tagline: the Shadow Owl who ushers in a new age
characters.quote: The true Instructional Nation shall rise again!
characters.title: “Shadow Owl” of Nine Earths
characters.body: He abandoned the Kau faction and placed his hopes in a new White Owl—Akau. He passed the White Owl badge to Akau. After clashing with Koto of Five Heavens, he died.
characters.comment: Arsha: “He was a rather formidable opponent. If Koto had not been there, it might have been dangerous.”

characters.name: Lakán Sài
characters.tagline: the young detective who died for justice
characters.quote: If I die here, justice dies too. I’m walking out of this room alive!
characters.title: Detective, First Investigations Division, Metropolitan Police
characters.body: Lai’s son. While pursuing the truth behind Kïràné’s assassination, he came to suspect Tané, but clashed with Akau, who sought to protect Tané, and lost his life. In heaven, he guides Mono.
characters.combat_style: Stance of Moderation: Kai / police baton
characters.comment: Koto: “He was a young man with a strong sense of justice. But if he obstructs my lord, then of course he deserves to die.”

characters.name: Majén Qàlé
characters.tagline: the Second Instructor who loves nuclear power and peace
characters.quote: Princess Namire—and Chairman Lai’s grandson as well. Your little act to buy time has gone on quite long enough.
characters.title: Second Instructor; student at the Narejia Federation University
characters.body: A childhood friend of Tané and Akau. He studied nuclear power at the Narejia Federation University and grew close to Satella, only to be betrayed by her. He later supported the nation as Second Instructor.
characters.comment: Arsha: “An Instructor who is a nuclear-power enthusiast—what an unusual person.”

characters.name: Ládén Tané
characters.tagline: the strong-armed leader who carries sorrow on his back
characters.quote: I will not become Pàlajé.
characters.title: Fifth First Instructor
characters.body: Kïràné’s son-in-law. After his wife Mono fell into a coma, he led Kàlavé to economic growth as a “strong-arm” ruler. He supported the nation together with Akau.
characters.comment: Koto: “Ah, my beloved lord! The most beautiful and precious person in all the world!”

characters.id: コト-テイル-アイテール
characters.name: Koto Tál Aitēl
characters.tagline: the blind murderous blade, incurably in love with her lord
characters.quote: I had wanted to kill him myself. It was worth coming here in person.
characters.title: President of Aitēl Company; “Blue Heaven” of Five Heavens; Tané’s former second wife
characters.body: Tané’s former second wife and the Blue Heaven of Five Heavens. Because of her twisted love for Tané, she tried to kill him and came into fierce conflict with Akau. She is blind, but her senses are extraordinarily sharp.
characters.combat_style: automatic counterattack / thrown knives · close combat
characters.comment: Arsha: “She truly is insane. Wanting to kill one’s favorite is beyond my understanding.”

characters.name: Satella Long
characters.tagline: the former witch still searching for happiness
characters.quote: Roger!
characters.title: Dorm mother of the Nolt student dormitory; former “witch” of Jūnā
characters.body: A member of the secret society Jūnā, she approached Qàlé, and later fought Akau and the others in Kàlavé. After her defeat, she reformed and became a dorm mother in the Har-Lopinong Republic.
characters.comment: Koto: “That woman’s speed was troublesome. But in the end she was only a loser.”

characters.name: Ládén Lā
characters.tagline: the strongest old man
characters.quote: Youth is force, but old age is experience.
characters.title: First Instructor (Republican era)
characters.body: The First Instructor of the Republican era and Kïnàbé’s father. Powerful enough to overwhelm Lei, he nevertheless watched the tide of the age pass and later taught the sword to Lai.
characters.combat_style: True Stance of Moderation, Stance of Gentleness / short blade (fririjin) · staff
characters.comment: Arsha: “An outrageous old gentleman. At his age, moving like that is unbelievable.”

characters.name: Ládén a Mono
characters.tagline: the kindhearted wife whom Tané loved
characters.quote: Politics is not about bringing someone down. It ought to create a place where everyone can sleep in peace, like this hospital room.
characters.title: Tané’s wife; daughter of Kïràné
characters.body: Kïràné’s daughter and Tané’s wife. Fed lies about the truth of her father’s death through Ètïré’s schemes, she fell into a coma, but awakened after a very long time.
characters.comment: Koto: “That woman! Strutting about as if she were my lord’s lawful wife... I wish she would disappear already!”

characters.id: シャウン-ア-エティレイ
characters.name: Shàun a Ètïré
characters.tagline: the former White Owl who set the stage for ruin
characters.quote: Kill me! Kill me, and you will become the new Lord Pàlajé!
characters.title: Member of Parliament; “White Owl”
characters.body: A woman from a cadet branch of the Shàun house. She enticed Tané and tried to have Kïràné assassinated. She sought to drag Tané down the path of carnage, failed, and was then assassinated by Apàla.
characters.comment: Arsha: “To plot a conspiracy is to stake your life on it. Her end was abrupt indeed.”

characters.name: Nálén Sālé
characters.tagline: the spring sun who designs Kàlavé’s economy
characters.quote: If His Holiness Tané is the winter bonfire, then I am the spring sun.
characters.title: Administrative Vice Minister of Commerce
characters.body: An elite official in charge of economic policy under the Tané administration. He clashed with Akau, and the two hurled their ideals against one another.
characters.comment: Koto: “An impertinent youngster. He only basks in my lord’s reflected glory.”

characters.id: キルロード-ドゥビ-セレスティア
characters.name: Killord Dubi Selestia
characters.tagline: Five Heavens’ long-suffering mediator
characters.quote: No good. His Holiness is scheduled to attend today as well. Like it or not, everyone will be present.
characters.title: “Yellow Heaven” of Five Heavens
characters.body: An executive of Five Heavens. The eighth legitimate son of the Celestia family, he was castrated, and now suffers endlessly trying to hold together his freewheeling fellow executives.
characters.comment: Arsha: “His Highness has it hard too. To keep together an organization full of eccentrics like us is no easy task.”

characters.id: アーシャ-プリティヴィティ
characters.name: Arsha Pritivitiĕ
characters.tagline: the Violet Heaven who adores double-entry bookkeeping
characters.quote: This is the true glory of double-entry bookkeeping!
characters.title: “Violet Heaven” of Five Heavens; CEO of Lavacuda Company
characters.body: CEO of a synthetic-fiber manufacturer in Worl and the chief money-earner of Five Heavens. A fervent fan of Tiana, she often acts together with Koto.
characters.comment: Koto: “A fake lady. She talks of nothing but business and has no refinement.”

characters.name: Alka Melduĕk
characters.tagline: the hollow god who stages tragedy
characters.quote: You were the protagonist. You blinded yourself with your own radiance.
characters.title: “Scarlet Heaven” of Five Heavens
characters.body: Leader of Five Heavens, and the one who transformed the organization into a radical religious criminal syndicate. Since his university days, he has been a Machiavellian who delights in manipulating the misfortune and ruin of others.
characters.comment: Arsha: “Though he is my lord, I sense an unfathomable terror in him.”

characters.name: Kybi Árin
characters.tagline: the cross-dressing madman who covets Latia’s seat
characters.quote: Eirin-chan just killed a whole bunch of annoying people, okay? Totally different from that old hag ♡
characters.title: “Green Heaven” of Five Heavens; chief of Qubi Frauzett
characters.body: The far-right executive of Five Heavens, fond of women’s dress. He schemes to usurp the seat of Scarlet Heaven and also clashes with Koto and the others.
characters.comment: Koto: “A filthy man! I shall carve him to pieces with my knife!”

characters.name: Ágre Yall
characters.tagline: the activist who has called himself a student for years
characters.quote: If you have learned the truth, then you shall become a sacrifice for the revolution here and now.
characters.title: Chairman and general secretary of Manzal
characters.body: Leader of Manzal, the student movement organization at Hellén National University. After killing a member of Five Heavens, he attacked Aliĕ and the others to conceal the crime.
characters.comment: Arsha: “A terrorist wearing the skin of a student. As the head of an organization, that is a failure.”

characters.name: Darc Slingar
characters.tagline: the spy who moves behind a cheerful face
characters.quote: I am a messenger of freedom. Young people, cast the dice with your own hands to your heart’s content!
characters.title: Har-Lopinong police officer; intelligence operative of the Worl Federation Preparatory Committee
characters.body: A police officer assigned to a neighborhood box in the Sósos district, but in truth an agent of Worl. He leaks information to the young and sets events in motion.
characters.comment: Koto: “A grimy intelligence agent. One day he will slip up.”

characters.name: Shiyuĕ Lei
characters.tagline: the mad scientist who fell from despair into insanity
characters.quote: I am trying to save lives.
characters.title: Physician at Sósos West Clinic; chief researcher at the National Institute of Medical Science
characters.body: A second-generation Solan immigrant physician. When approval for a Glück virus treatment would not come, he used an unapproved drug to save Dahia’s life, and later cast ethics aside and became a mad scientist.
characters.comment: Arsha: “What a dreadful world, for a doctor to administer poison.”

characters.name: Mon Harc
characters.tagline: the poster girl who serves nothing but fried rice
characters.quote: Fried rice. Four. Done.
characters.title: Poster girl of Yifun Restaurant; Yuum’s younger sister
characters.body: A second-generation Solan working at Yifun Restaurant. Her Aldian is halting, and she ignores the order and serves fried rice for everything. Later, she sets her sights on Hellén National University.
characters.comment: Koto: “Nothing but fried rice... the nutrition will become unbalanced.”

characters.id: シナン-アルスノーヴァ
characters.name: Shinan Arsnorva
characters.tagline: the hot-blooded hero who protects Sósos
characters.quote: My name is Shinan Arsnorva! One of Five Heavens’ Fàrned—servant of Lady Scarlet Heaven—and the impossibly cool hero of this city!
characters.title: Member of Five Heavens (Fàrned); hero
characters.body: Though one of the moderates within Five Heavens, she also acts as a hero protecting peace in the city. She is Minatsu’s elder sister.
characters.combat_style: Arsnova Punch (joint locks and hand-to-hand combat)
characters.comment: Arsha: “A hero though she is in the mafia—what a contradiction.”

characters.name: Leto Lówa
characters.tagline: the grand chancellor sent from the Ministry for the Preservation of Monarchy
characters.quote: I hid my true identity from all of you. And I became Grand Chancellor. Do you understand what it means to say that to me?
characters.title: Student at Hellén National University; Grand Chancellor of the Eltēna Union
characters.body: A young man who studied abroad in Hellén together with Aliĕ as her minder. Gifted in strategy, he served as the club’s chief brain.
characters.comment: Koto: “For a grand chancellor to pretend to be a student—what an idle person.”

characters.name: Aliĕ Namile
characters.tagline: the wise princess who keeps her eyes on the future
characters.quote: “Do not eliminate the street—evolve the street.”
characters.title: Law student at Hellén National University; Princess of Reneti
characters.body: A princess of the Eltēna Union who nevertheless studied in Hellén and matched Hal in debate. She grows together with Fata as the two are drawn to one another.
characters.comment: Arsha: “For a royal daughter to wave an air gun around in the street—she is quite a tomboy.”

characters.name: Lakán Fàta
characters.tagline: the pure-white swordsman who inherits his grandfather’s will
characters.quote: I am one who will bring choice back to Kàlavé. You have already chosen to protect someone. One day, learn to make the ‘choice’ to erase an enemy with your own hands.
characters.title: Student at Hellén National University
characters.body: Son of Lakán Sài and grandson of Lai. Inheriting his grandfather’s swordsmanship, he joins Aliĕ, Minatsu, and the others in Hellén to confront conspiracy.
characters.combat_style: Stance of Savagery, Stance of No-Mind / wooden sword · Pàlajé’s Knife
characters.comment: Koto: “So another of that bloodline has appeared. How irritating.”

characters.id: ミナツ-アルスノーヴァ
characters.name: Minac Arsnorva
characters.tagline: an otaku girl and practitioner of the Stance of Moderation
characters.quote: Die and apologize, you bastard!
characters.title: President of the West Altos Researcher’s Club
characters.body: Sinan’s younger sister. Usually an otaku girl, but highly skilled with the sword. She fights beside Fata using Pàlajé’s Knife as her weapon.
characters.combat_style: Stance of Moderation / wooden sword · Pàlajé’s Knife
characters.comment: Arsha: “A swordsman who draws dōjinshi... quite a traffic jam of attributes.”

characters.name: Kura Mogroi
characters.tagline: the Durgu songstress whose khoomei resounds
characters.quote: O lone wolf who steps upon the land of Asetiro—
characters.title: Student at Hellén National University; street musician
characters.body: An international student from Durguatan. Through street performances, she supports Aliĕ and the others with song and khoomei.
characters.comment: Koto: “What a barbaric singing voice. It will sully my lord’s ears.”

characters.name: Hal Perort
characters.tagline: the gluttonous debater who possesses both logic and feeling
characters.quote: I want to protect the future in order to protect everyone’s today.
characters.title: Student at Hellén National University
characters.body: A master debater and Aliĕ’s rival. He is always eating vast amounts of sweets, yet in argument his power of persuasion is overwhelming.
characters.comment: Arsha: “How does he eat that much and not get fat? I envy him.”

characters.name: Tor Secmet
characters.tagline: the extremist who subdues the majority through violence
characters.quote: This is how I won my place—through “power.” You did the same, didn’t you?
characters.title: Fàrned of Five Heavens
characters.body: Driven toward violence by a past of persecution in Kàlavé, he opposed Sinan as an extremist within Five Heavens. Koto cut him down without mercy.
characters.comment: Koto: “Anyone incompetent enough to hold me back, I cut away without mercy.”

characters.name: Dahia Opfar
characters.tagline: the kindhearted girl toyed with by tragedy
characters.quote: I love this club so much. But I have to support my family.
characters.title: Former member of the West Altos Researcher’s Club
characters.body: Once a central figure in the club, she took leave from school because her family fell into hardship. She later contracted the Glück virus and wandered at the border of life and death.
characters.comment: Arsha: “To give up her dream for her family—what a pitiable child.”

characters.name: Els Ma N'Garsha
characters.tagline: the bare-knuckle royal
characters.quote: Cut the nonsense! Pour that surging feeling into your limbs!
characters.title: Graduate student at Hellén National University; royal of Deltai
characters.body: A royal of Deltai’s Ma N'Garsha dynasty. Admired by Sinan, he supported the club together with Yuum.
characters.combat_style: bare-knuckle fighting · strikes · kicks / wooden sword
characters.comment: Koto: “For royalty to stoop to grilling meat... how far she has fallen.”

characters.name: Yuĕm Harc
characters.tagline: the weary man who protected the club to the very end
characters.quote: In trying so hard to protect the club as a whole, I overlooked what mattered most.
characters.title: Second president of the West Altos Researcher’s Club; Mon’s elder brother
characters.body: Mon’s brother. A master of swordsmanship who rebuilt the club, only to see it driven into suspension by Arka’s schemes.
characters.comment: Arsha: “Because her sense of responsibility was so strong, she ended up tormenting herself.”

characters.id: エスティ-シュメルマルク
characters.name: Estiĕ Shmelmalk
characters.tagline: the young man who loved Arka and fell into darkness
characters.quote: Whatever you may be plotting, I will bear that sin beside you!
characters.title: Young florist
characters.body: Drawn to Arka, he threw himself into the mafia for her sake. He tried to shoulder her crimes together with her.
characters.comment: Koto: “Taking on the crimes of the one you love... in that one small way, she resembles my lord.”

characters.name: Jen D'Ark
characters.tagline: the deranged legislator who would cull the unenlightened
characters.quote: A few years from now, opposition to immigrants will be the natural public mood. But that will be too late. This is the moment of “choice”: seize the age before it comes, or be flattened by it.
characters.title: Moderate Party legislator
characters.body: Working with Shiyuĕ, she spread “Auditio” and committed terrorism in an effort to force human evolution onward. She was defeated by Satella.
characters.comment: Arsha: “A politician who is a terrorist—Har-Lopinong must truly be in its final days.”

characters.id: ロイド-ゴーストピース
characters.name: Loid Gorstpiĕss
characters.tagline: the foreign student whose heart aches over Deltai’s civil war
characters.quote: I’m sick of this farce already!
characters.title: Student at Hellén National University
characters.body: An international student from Deltai. Agonized by his homeland’s civil war, he fell into despair and self-destruction, but was supported by his friends.
characters.comment: Koto: “To wail over something as minor as a civil war—how weak.”

characters.name: Tiana Mellén
characters.tagline: the Machiavellian genius adored by Arsha
characters.quote: Thank you for waiting, Lady Pritivitiĕ, Lady Aitēl. I am Tiana Mellén, president of Enimuĕc.
characters.title: President of Enimyuts Company; leader of Bai Cong Hui
characters.body: Arsha’s junior from their university days, and her idol. Behind the scenes, however, she is also a mafia boss, with a cold nature that toys effortlessly with others.
characters.comment: Arsha: “Ah, Lady Tiana! As beautiful and brilliant as ever!”

characters.name: Pājén・■
characters.tagline: the hunter Pàlé’s child who heralds the end
characters.quote: I am Pājén・■ (Doa), child of the hunter Pàlé. This is the beginning of the Circular End. People who yielded to Bïàlé, promptly return the earth to me.
characters.title: First Instructor of the Final Kàlavé Instructional Nation
characters.body: A supernatural being who appears in an “if” world line, declares the founding of Kàlavé’s Final Instructional Nation, and inaugurates the apocalyptic war called the “Circular End.” It can rule over human subconsciousness and erase whole spaces outright. After battling Salei, it acknowledges the appeal of the “false book” (the what-if world), and later comes to be called “Door” as well.
characters.combat_style: supernatural powers (spatial erasure, subconscious domination) / fox-colored knife (special)
characters.comment: Koto: “She uses incomprehensible psychic powers. But in the end she seemed almost like nothing more than an insolent child, and it threw me off.”

characters.name: Tággén Agaké
characters.tagline: the Shadow Owl of Nine Earths who upheld the Final Instructional Nation
characters.quote: The easier attacks slip outside deliberate thought, the easier they are to land.
characters.title: “Shadow Owl” of Nine Earths; Third Instructor of the Final Kàlavé Instructional Nation
characters.body: A man who served as Nine Earths’ Shadow Owl under the Patei regime. With Door’s advent, he abandoned Patei and became the Third Instructor of the Final Instructional Nation. Door seized his subconscious and made him fight Satella, Akau, and the others, but he was freed by the ritual of dismissal carried out through the instructors’ deliberation.
characters.comment: Arsha: “To have one’s unconscious manipulated is the height of trouble. Still, I am glad he was freed in the end.”

knives.name: Pure-White Knife
knives.tagline: the blade passed down from Kïnàbé to Lai
knives.body: A knife handed down from Káin Kàna to Ládén Kïnàbé, then from Lakán Lài to Akau. Painted white, it is later stained with Akau’s blood.
knives.comment: Arsha: “The color pure white expresses the cleanliness of their ideals.”

knives.name: Crimson Knife
knives.tagline: the sword engraved with the crest of House Bïláin
knives.body: A sword coated in the crimson lacquer that symbolizes House Bïláin. It bears the inscription, “May it be stained with blood no further.” It was wielded by Àkané and Tané.
knives.comment: Koto: “It is the knife my lord carried! Crimson suited him very well!”

knives.name: Fox-Colored Knife
knives.tagline: the knife Pàlajé used throughout his life
knives.body: A double-edged straight blade (zïrïjin) with the crest of House Shàun on the hilt and fox-colored ornamentation reserved for the First Instructor alone. Both genuine and counterfeit versions exist, and it was used by Minatsu and Fata as well.
knives.comment: Arsha: “How ironic that a three-way struggle should arise over this knife.”

knives.name: Jet-Black Knife
knives.tagline: the murderous blade used by those who sank into darkness
knives.body: A blackened knife associated with those who walked the path of conspiracy and terror. Lei and Jen were among its wielders.
knives.comment: Koto: “It is the knife Lady Lei used. The jet-black blade is very beautiful.”

stances.name: Stance of Forthrightness
stances.body: A sword style prized in the bloodline of House Naren, built on direct and explosive advances. Its hallmark is overwhelming power.
stances.comment: Arsha: “A way of fighting by sheer force is refreshing to watch.”

stances.name: Stance of Gentleness
stances.tagline: willow-like defense that diverts a raging current
stances.body: A form handed down in House Ládén. Rather than receiving an enemy’s attack head-on, it brushes it aside from the flank and excels at defense that creates an opening.
stances.comment: Koto: “All that running around is dull. I wish it would show me more blood.”

stances.name: Stance of Savagery
stances.tagline: unpredictable chains of attack driven by instinct
stances.body: A form that uses anger and emotion as its propulsion, bewildering the enemy with unreadable movement. It throws away defense and specializes in killing.
stances.comment: Arsha: “A way of fighting that bares its emotions—truly beastly.”

stances.name: Stance of No-Mind
stances.tagline: a form that strips away hesitation and waste
stances.body: A stance that casts off distraction, hesitation, and excess in order to cut with absolute economy. Calm judgment and simple finishing strokes define it.
stances.comment: Koto: “No-mind leaves not a fragment of humanity. Though when my lord uses it, it is wonderful!”

stances.name: Stance of Moderation: Prelude
stances.tagline: the opening form that balances offense and defense
stances.body: The foundational form of the Stance of Moderation, seeking balance rather than excess. It aims to answer the enemy with the minimum necessary movement and force.
stances.comment: Arsha: “It shows that balance is what matters in all things.”

stances.name: True Stance of Moderation
stances.tagline: the perfected balance reached only by masters
stances.body: A perfected form attained only by the highest masters of moderation. It harmonizes offense, defense, judgment, and timing at an extraordinary level.
stances.comment: Koto: “Only a tiny handful can master this. It is truly the work of a genius.”

stances.name: Stance of Moderation: Kai
stances.tagline: a practical revision fused with riot-control technique
stances.body: A revised and highly practical form developed by En Nèkïné. By fusing Duïlé with police riot-control methods, it is optimized for real combat and arrests alike.
stances.comment: Arsha: “A practical improvement befitting the police. Efficient and splendid.”

stances.name: Stance of Revolving Flame
stances.tagline: a whirling style that burns through openings
stances.body: A stance in which circular movement and sudden ignition-like bursts combine. It presses in from shifting angles and burns through the enemy’s guard.
stances.comment: Koto: “Enduring and bearing with things is impossible for me. I would want to cut them to pieces at once.”

terms.title: Kàlavatan Republic
terms.body: A republican state that preceded the Kàlavé Instructional Nation. Centered on the president, the Council, and Parliament, it was later dismantled by Pàlajé.

terms.title: Kàlavé Instructional Nation
terms.body: A state that adopts the Dírál faction as its official creed. It binds religious authority and secular rule tightly together under the First Instructor.

terms.title: Council
terms.body: A powerful institution within the Kàlavatan Republic. Leading figures, including men from Kïnàiny, gathered there and exerted great influence over national policy.

terms.title: Parliament
terms.body: The legislative body that deliberates on laws (secular law) and submits them to the instructors. From the Republic’s final years into the early Instructional Nation, it became the main battlefield of politics.

terms.title: Teaching Synod
terms.body: The highest religious deliberative body, which debates norms (religious law) and submits them to the instructors. It also takes part in selecting the First Instructor.

terms.title: First Instructor
terms.body: The highest instructional office and the head of state of the Kàlavé Instructional Nation. It possesses immense authority in both secular rule and religious prestige.

terms.title: Nine Clans
terms.body: The collective name for Kàlavé’s nine great houses. They are closely tied to the succession of the First Instructor and to the tradition of the Child of Destiny.

terms.title: Kïnàiny
terms.body: The knightly and armed order directly under the Kàlavatan Republic. Rich in masters of Duïlé, it functioned as the Republic’s instrument of force.

terms.title: Nine Earths
terms.body: A secret organization that works in the shadows behind Kàlavé’s political and religious struggles. It harbored the Shadow Owl and White Owl, and played a deep role in founding the Instructional Nation.

terms.title: Republican Alliance
terms.body: The ruling bloc of the Republic and successor to the Nationalist Alliance. It feared the Child of Destiny and wielded strong influence over how Pàlajé was handled.

terms.title: Ismizlark Alliance
terms.body: A right-wing political force backed by conservative Dírál believers. It championed defense of the sacred land and Instructionalism, and grew powerful in the Republic’s final political struggles.

terms.title: National Self-Determination Party
terms.body: A right-wing force advocating escape from Armona colonialism. Alongside the Ismizlark Alliance and others, it supported the coalition governments of the Republic’s last days.

terms.title: Confederated States Army
terms.body: The collective name for the forces of “orthodox” believers and ethnic minorities backed by Niqeltoliĕnē. They fought the Instructional Nation in the battles of the Xāváléqï Corridor.

terms.title: Ismizlark
terms.body: The name of the religious system centered on Ilat, the sole god of the earth, and Mazgūk, the final prophet. It exerts enormous influence on politics, culture, and law across the Kàlavé world.

terms.title: Dírál faction
terms.body: A branch of Ismizlark that emphasizes the instructors’ revelatory authority to interpret the law. It is the state creed of the Kàlavé Instructional Nation.

terms.title: Child of Destiny
terms.body: The being believed to bear the fate of restoring the Instructional Nation. It is tied to the tradition of a “child born left-handed” into the Nine Clans.

terms.title: Hāké
terms.body: The capital of Kàlavé. From the Republican era through the Instructional Nation, it functions as the center of both politics and religion.

terms.title: Xāváléqï
terms.body: The sacred land of Kàlavé. Known as a hot-spring resort and pilgrimage site, it is an area of immense religious and military importance.

terms.title: Xāváléqï Corridor
terms.body: The corridor zone surrounding the sacred land amid the mountain ranges. Centered on Xāváléqï, it is a strategic crossroads of religion, transport, and war.

terms.title: Zopāitān City
terms.body: A city known as the stronghold of Nine Earths. It is where Pàlajé was taken after his abduction and where battles between the Republic and Nine Earths were fought.

terms.title: Làitéqï Village
terms.body: A village belonging to Zoruā City. It is Èshe’s home village, depicted as a place where the old custom of night-crawling courtship still remains.

terms.title: Jïrīupēr Province
terms.body: The province that includes Zoruā City. It is the region where Pàlajé and Kïnàbé urged legal reforms to preserve public morals according to Dírál precepts.

terms.title: Ládéqï
terms.body: The largest city in Jïrīupēr Province. It is also the electoral base of Shàun Pàlé.

terms.title: Bïlàitān City
terms.body: The capital of Káshatìléqï Province. Its fall became the trigger for the plan to reorganize the state into the Instructional Nation.

terms.title: Yìlà
terms.body: Kàlavé’s largest city. It prospered as the economic center of the coast and later became a key hub for triport construction.

terms.title: Duram Sea
terms.body: The sea to the west of Kàlavé. It has a major influence on the economy and diplomacy of the coastal regions.

terms.title: Misenitiu Grand Auditorium
terms.body: A great auditorium counted among Kàlavé’s important ceremonial and political spaces.

terms.title: Bridge of Conversion
terms.body: The bridge where conviction and political choice collide. It is remembered as the site of Pàlajé and Kïnàbé’s fatal confrontation.

terms.title: Gatina Grand Auditorium
terms.body: A grand assembly hall tied to Kàlavé’s politics and public ceremonies.

terms.title: Great Temple of Friya
terms.body: A great temple that stands as one of the major sacred structures of Kàlavé.

terms.title: Mavwa Grand Auditorium
terms.body: One of the major grand auditoriums used in Kàlavé’s public and political life.

terms.title: Har-Lopinong Republic
terms.body: A republic distinct from Kàlavé and the stage of the second major storyline. University life, street politics, mafia struggles, and immigrant issues intersect there.

terms.title: Durguatan
terms.body: A foreign land and cultural sphere associated with steppe culture and throat singing.

terms.title: Armona
terms.body: A great power whose colonialism and military pressure weigh heavily on the Kàlavatan Republic.

terms.title: Worl
terms.body: A foreign country deeply entangled in the politics, business, and intelligence wars of later eras.

terms.title: Worl Federation
terms.body: The federal state centered on Worl. It influences other countries through politics, capital, and covert operations.

terms.title: Deltai
terms.body: A foreign country beset by civil war and dynastic conflict.

terms.title: Jaxadarn
terms.body: A protectorate on the Kàlavé frontier associated with orthodox believers and international conflict.

terms.title: Valaria
terms.body: A foreign power or region that appears in the international politics of the setting.

terms.title: Western Tinebris
terms.body: A western cultural sphere known for its martial arts and scholarship, both of which Kàna taught to Pàlajé.

terms.title: Niqeltoliĕnē
terms.body: A great power opposed to Armona. It supports forces hostile to the Republic and shapes the wider war.

terms.title: Narejia Federation
terms.body: A federation whose universities and advanced learning play a part in the later story.

terms.title: Hellén
terms.body: A major foreign city and academic center where many of the Har-Lopinong storyline’s students gather.

terms.title: Sósos district
terms.body: A district of the Har-Lopinong Republic. Clinics, police boxes, and street communities there play important roles in the story.

terms.title: Elben district
terms.body: A district tied to the political and social tensions of the Har-Lopinong Republic.

terms.title: Eltz City
terms.body: A city that appears in the Har-Lopinong storyline and the networks of business and society around it.

terms.title: Kininarabei City
terms.body: A city in the wider setting whose name evokes Kàlavé’s extended world.

terms.title: Jūnā
terms.body: A secret society or underground network whose members operate from the shadows.

terms.title: West Altos Researcher’s Club
terms.body: A university circle whose members become central figures in the Har-Lopinong storyline.

terms.title: Hellén Mafia
terms.body: The mafia operating in Hellén. It is involved in turf wars, underground business, and the struggles around Five Heavens.

terms.title: Aitēl Company
terms.body: The company headed by Koto Tál Aitēl.

terms.title: Enimyuts Company
terms.body: The company headed by Tiana Mellén.

terms.title: Realia Company
terms.body: A company that appears in the later Har-Lopinong storyline.

terms.title: Lavacuda Company
terms.body: The company led by Arsha Pritivitiĕ, known for chemical fibers and large profits.

terms.title: Skiel Company
terms.body: A company active in the economic and corporate side of the setting.

terms.title: Eltz Company
terms.body: A company bearing the name of Eltz and tied to the story’s corporate sphere.

terms.title: Nolt
terms.body: The name of the student dormitory where Satella later serves as dorm mother.

terms.title: Hellén National University
terms.body: The university where many major figures of the Har-Lopinong storyline study.

terms.title: Gatina Grand Auditorium
terms.body: A grand assembly hall tied to Kàlavé’s politics and public ceremonies.

terms.title: Misenitiu Grand Auditorium
terms.body: A great auditorium counted among Kàlavé’s important ceremonial and political spaces.

terms.title: Mavwa Grand Auditorium
terms.body: One of the major grand auditoriums used in Kàlavé’s public and political life.

terms.id: ハヴァレイズィ大講堂
terms.title: Xāválézï Grand Auditorium
terms.body: A grand auditorium in the Kàlavé world, tied to public ritual and political space.

terms.title: Emeran Grand Auditorium
terms.body: A grand auditorium that appears among the important halls of the setting.

terms.title: Sazani Grand Auditorium
terms.body: Another of the setting’s major grand auditoriums.

terms.title: Riunisheben Base
terms.body: A military base important to the later political and military story.

terms.id: タネイ・トライポート
terms.title: Tané Triport
terms.body: A large triport project associated with Yìlà and Tané’s era.

terms.title: Lurupe Accord
terms.body: An accord or treaty that plays a role in the political order of the later setting.

terms.title: Police Agency
terms.body: The national police authority. In the age of terrorism and political disorder, it oversees criminal investigations and anti-terror operations.

terms.title: Ismizlark Alliance
terms.body: A right-wing political force backed by conservative Dírál believers. It championed defense of the sacred land and Instructionalism, and grew powerful in the Republic’s final political struggles.

terms.title: Nia Kïnàiny
terms.body: A name associated with Kïnàiny’s institutional legacy and later developments.

terms.title: Confederated States Army
terms.body: The collective name for the forces of “orthodox” believers and ethnic minorities backed by Niqeltoliĕnē. They fought the Instructional Nation in the battles of the Xāváléqï Corridor.
